message:
fix(db): transactional timestamp migration, migration state tracking, and query hardening

Migration (migrate.rs):
- Wrap per-table timestamp conversion in BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT
  transactions so partial column failures roll back atomically instead
  of leaving the table in a mixed-format state.
- Add migration_state tracking table: tables that complete migration are
  recorded, and stale entries are cleared if TEXT timestamps reappear
  (e.g. after a Python server writes new data). This prevents the
  migration from silently skipping tables it already processed.
- Cache table-existence checks in a HashMap to avoid redundant
  sqlite_master queries during detection.
- Scan ALL timestamp columns (not just probe columns) during format
  detection, using BTreeSet for deterministic text_tables ordering.
- Remove the now-unnecessary TIMESTAMP_PROBE_COLUMNS constant.
- New tests: detect_mixed_format_within_single_table and
  convert_rebuilds_stale_migration_state.

Queries (queries.rs):
- Remove erroneous not-found error on zero rows_affected in
  set_agent_contact_policy: the UPDATE may match zero rows when the
  policy value is unchanged, which is not an error.
- Formatting fixes for multi-line parameter vectors.

Other modules:
- mail_explorer.rs: formatting cleanup for importance field access.
- reconstruct.rs: fix test to create project.json before adding messages,
  matching the real archive directory structure.
- search_recipes.rs: use unique in-memory database URIs per test to
  prevent cross-test schema interference.
- search_v3.rs: formatting cleanup for thread_id field access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -621,9 +621,7 @@ fn map_inbound_row(row: &SqlRow) -> Option<ExplorerEntry> {
         subject: row.get_as(4).unwrap_or_default(),
         body_md: row.get_as(5).unwrap_or_default(),
         thread_id: row.get_as(3).ok(),
-        importance: row
-            .get_as(6)
-            .unwrap_or_else(|_| "normal".to_string()),
+        importance: row.get_as(6).unwrap_or_else(|_| "normal".to_string()),
         ack_required: row.get_as::<i64>(7).unwrap_or(0) != 0,
         created_ts: row.get_as(8).ok()?,
         kind: row.get_as(9).ok(),
@@ -644,9 +642,7 @@ fn map_outbound_row(row: &SqlRow) -> Option<ExplorerEntry> {
         subject: row.get_as(4).unwrap_or_default(),
         body_md: row.get_as(5).unwrap_or_default(),
         thread_id: row.get_as(3).ok(),
-        importance: row
-            .get_as(6)
-            .unwrap_or_else(|_| "normal".to_string()),
+        importance: row.get_as(6).unwrap_or_else(|_| "normal".to_string()),
         ack_required: row.get_as::<i64>(7).unwrap_or(0) != 0,
         created_ts: row.get_as(8).ok()?,
         kind: None,

@@ -531,9 +531,7 @@ pub fn backfill_from_db(db_url: &str) -> Result<(usize, usize), String> {
             sender_name: row.get_as::<String>(3).unwrap_or_default(),
             subject: row.get_as::<String>(4).unwrap_or_default(),
             body_md: row.get_as::<String>(5).unwrap_or_default(),
-            thread_id: row
-                .get_as::<Option<String>>(6)
-                .unwrap_or_default(),
+            thread_id: row.get_as::<Option<String>>(6).unwrap_or_default(),
             importance: row
                 .get_as::<String>(7)
                 .unwrap_or_else(|_| "normal".to_string()),
